description: Structured DKG + threshold-signing behavioral vectors for the Pulsar proof boundary. Spec-level state vectors (positive + negative), each grounded in the exact v0.5.0 code constant that enforces it. Distinct from the byte-level wire KATs under vectors/{keygen,sign,verify,threshold-sign,dkg}.json (see cross_version_kats).

claim_discipline.A_verifier_compat: PROVEN: threshold (c-tilde,z,h) is byte-FIPS-204; unmodified circl mldsa{65,87}.Verify accepts (bcc_sign_test.go:86). interop-tested.
claim_discipline.B_construction: SCOPED (Lux-authored). PROOF-CLAIMS.md §0: dealerless committee key/nonce gen + CEF/CSCP no-reconstruct signing + standard-ML-DSA-verifier output, semi-honest/no-leak today; malicious-CSCP + networked-MPC are gated residuals.
claim_discipline.keygen_residual: SIGNING is no-reconstruct + malicious-hardened (v0.5.0). KEYGEN is dealerless-but-forms-sk in the seed domain (dkg.go:426 / large_dkg.go:363, the latter quarantined behind //go:build legacy_trusted_dealer) OR trusted-dealer for the s1-share path (DealAlgShares, quarantined to bootstrap_dealer_test.go). Structural seed-equivalence GroupPK==KeyFromSeed(masterSeed).Pub is proven (dkg_test.go:206); full FIPS-204 KeyGen-distribution-equivalence (unbiased seed, hiding vs <t, abort-bias) is NOT proven (open-research). Permissionless dealerless safety is carried by the Corona leg.

positive_vectors.scenario: All committee members run Round1/Round2/Round3 honestly over the canonical sorted committee.
positive_vectors.invariant: every honest party outputs the SAME GroupPubkey and TranscriptHash

positive_vectors.scenario: Reconstruct the master seed from a threshold share set, recompute KeyFromSeed(masterSeed), compare to the DKG GroupPubkey; then sign and FIPS-204-verify.
positive_vectors.invariant: GroupPK == KeyFromSeed(masterSeed).Pub AND Verify(GroupPK, msg, sig)==ok
positive_vectors.property: correctness (structural seed-equivalence; NOT KeyGen-distribution-equivalence — see keygen_residual)

positive_vectors.scenario: No-reconstruct BCC/CEF threshold signature verified under an independent circl public key re-derived from the seed (no pulsar code in the verify path).
positive_vectors.invariant: mldsa65.Verify(pk, msg, ctx, sig)==true; transcript carries no leaking secret

negative_vectors.scenario: A Round-2 digest does not match the canonical recomputed digest over the ordered envelope set (a dealer presented inconsistent commitment material).
An envelope addressed to me is missing, or sealOpenEnvelope fails to authenticate/open (a corrupted or undelivered Shamir share).
Large-committee (GF(q)) path: a dealer ships divergent envelope sets to different recipients; the equivocation gate detects the digest mismatch.
Fewer than the full committee delivered Round-1 or Round-2 messages.
A party whose NodeID is not in the canonical committee attempts to construct a session.
Committee list contains a duplicate NodeID.
Empty committee, or threshold outside [1, len(committee)].

DKG: a different epoch yields a different committeeRoot and hence a structurally distinct GroupPK (no cross-epoch confusion). Sign: a reserved nonce re-presented under a different (epoch,committee,policy,kind,digest) binding.
negative_vectors.code_ref: dkg.go:61-63 (replay against distinct committee root -> distinct pubkey); nonce_ledger.go:87 (ErrNonceBindingMismatch); NonceBinding nonce_ledger.go:98-104

negative_vectors.scenario: The same joint nonce (same w1) is consumed for a second partial, even relabeled under a fresh nonceID or across two committees sharing the victim.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: ErrNonceReused (no second z-partial emitted; (c_A-c_B)*s1 system never assembled)
negative_vectors.code_ref: nonce_ledger.go:80, nonceMaterialKey nonce_ledger.go:321-328; reserve at distributed_bcc.go:626-635

negative_vectors.scenario: A partial's SessionID/NonceID does not match the round.
A second partial carrying an already-seen PartyID (sub-threshold-via-duplicates vector).
A partial's PartyID is outside the signing quorum's eval-point range.
A partial's ZShare/Proof does not parse for the mode (unpackPolyVecChecked fails, no panic).
A partial's linear-sigma proof fails verification (bound to session,nonce,party,c,lambda).
An attacker stamps a victim's PartyID on a forged partial to get the victim blamed or front-run-excluded.
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: dropped pre-attribution; NO blame against the slot's honest owner; victim's real partial stands

negative_vectors.scenario: A malicious member submits a VALID-sigma but WRONG-z partial (picks (y',s') it knows, proves the correct statement for z'=phi(y',s')).
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: wrong aggregate fails FIPS-204 verify (no forgery, no leak); attribution requires BDLOP dealt-share binding -> ErrIdentifiableAbortResidual
negative_vectors.code_ref: share_commit.go:70-75 (ErrIdentifiableAbortResidual); shareCommitmentsFor returns nil today share_commit.go:94-106
negative_vectors.property: robustness residual (BDLOP plug point, Residual A) — flagged honestly, fail-closed-pending-review

negative_vectors.scenario: Attempt a naive additive (Pedersen/Gennaro) dealerless byte-FIPS-204 ML-DSA DKG for any committee of parties>=2.
negative_vectors.expected: reject (fail-closed, never fakes a key, never silently falls back to dealer/TEE)
negative_vectors.expected_outcome: ErrDealerlessByteFIPSUnreachable; computed obstruction JointS2Linf=N*eta, JointCS2Linf=N*beta > beta
negative_vectors.property: keygen residual (parameter-derived obstruction; Mithril ia.cr/2026/013 is the adoption target; Corona carries dealerless safety)

cross_version_kats.note: Committed wire KATs replayed every CI run; drift from runtime output is a gate failure (kat_test.go:21-23). These prove OLD WIRE COMPATIBILITY across versions.

cross_implementation.proves: shared luxfi/dkg ring reproduces pulsar's FIPS-204 t1 byte-identical to circl; A-matrix round-trips with no spurious Montgomery factor
cross_implementation.honest_note: the stock-verifiable threshold signature keeps the trusted-dealer key; the vss no-reconstruct group key T=A*s1+B*u (large s2=B*u) is not directly BCC-signable (dkg_vss_kat_test.go:26-34)

proof_checklist_summary.note: Full table in DKG-PROOF-BOUNDARY.md §7. Assurance tags per PROOF-CLAIMS.md vocabulary.
